Ireland were deemed to be over the hill after failing to turn up against France and scraping past Italy, but their demolition of England saw them return to their best.

Scotland's 50-40 victory over France last weekend was entirely unexpected - and breathtaking to watch

Wales prop Rhys Carre went viral on social media after his stunning try against Ireland in Dublin

Going into this weekend’s ‘Super Saturday’, France, Scotland and Ireland all remain in title contention. France and Scotland – who could lift the trophy for the first time since its expansion – are locked together on 16 points. Ireland are two adrift.

Italy could yet finish as high as third and England bottom. An English defeat in Paris will certainly see them record their worst tournament finish in history.

While this represents significant trouble for Borthwick, his players and all those with a love for English rugby, the fact there have been so many shocks and there is still so much to play for on the final weekend is exactly why we all tune in to watch professional sport.

Round four, in particular, also included some special individual moments – Wales prop Rhys Carre’s stunning 30-metre try in Dublin (which has since been viewed over five million times on TikTok alone), Tommaso Menoncello’s Gladiatorial-like Roman powerplay and Alex Mann’s mammoth 32 tackles.

2026 SIX NATIONS Team W L PD BP Pts 1. France 3 1 +79 4 16 2. Scotland 3 1 +21 4 16 3. Ireland 3 1 +16 2 14 4. Italy 2 2 -24 1 9 5. England 1 3 +4 2 6 6. Wales 0 4 -96 1 1

Such has been the topsy-turvy nature of this year’s Championship that if England and Wales played again on Saturday, another huge upset could well take place. Borthwick’s men would probably still be favourites, but the match would likely be a lot tighter.

It just goes to show how much can change in professional sport over the course of a month. And that unpredictability is exactly what keeps us all coming back for more.

First played as the Home Nations in 1883, the Six Nations provides a vital bridge between the January blues and the start of spring.

It represents rugby nirvana for all those who love the sport and is also the tournament that attracts the most casual fans.

My word have we been treated to some fine action this year. Long may it continue. The Six Nations has always been one of the jewels in rugby’s crown. But it has been so good this year, it will take some beating for the award of greatest sporting competition of all.

Sports.title: Iranian women's soccer team flies out of Australia - but more choose to stay as claim emerges of players' families taken hostage in their home country
Sports.content:
The remainder of the Iranian women's soccer team have departed Sydney for the Middle East - but it's understood more team members have been granted asylum.

Trouble for the women began last Monday, March 2, when they refused to sing Iran's national anthem before a match against South Korea on the Gold Coast during the Asia Cup - just days after the conflict between the US and Iran broke out.

Australia offered the teammates humanitarian visas after word of their protest reached Iran, triggering major backlash.

One conservative commentator in Iran labelled the group 'wartime traitors' and called for harsh punishment, fuelling fears the women would be persecuted if they returned home.

Five players accepted the visa offer soon after Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke visited their Gold Coast hotel. The five women split with the rest of the team, who travelled to Sydney on Tuesday evening to catch a flight out of the country.

Daily Mail understands one player and one procurement manager have since accepted asylum and did not board the Malaysia Airlines plane when it left at about 10.45pm Tuesday, following a lengthy delay at the gate.

'The situation for these individuals is that they had to make their own decision about their lives,' Burke told reporters on Tuesday night.

'I understand completely that Australians want to help. People kept saying publicly, ‘Can’t you just make sure at some point they’re away from it?’

The Iranian women's soccer team have left Australia (pictured are the women disembarking a flight from the Gold Coast to Sydney to catch a transfer bus to their international flight)

Mohadeseh Zolfi, aged in her 20s, is one of two players who accepted an offer of asylum after the original five, and was not on the plane out of Australia last night.

It is unclear what happened to Golnoosh Khosravi, above

'What we could do was make sure they were away from anyone connected to the Iranian authorities so they could make the phone calls they wanted.

'They could be told there was an offer. And if they wanted to reach us, they could do exactly that.

Burke praised the work of Home Affairs officials who have worked with the players in recent days.

'They were there in that situation face to face with women dealing with challenges that are extraordinary,' he said.

'I couldn't be prouder of the officials. But as I say, what mattered was that a choice was given and that it was given in a true situation of there being no pressure.

'I respect all the comments and concerns that people have had, as there's been protests around people being moved off and on buses.

'But what I want to say is that people going through had an opportunity with a Home Affairs official and no pressure around them to make their own decision.

'And I respect their independence and agency with whatever decision they choose.

Team procurement officer Fleur Meshkin-Kar was also not on the flight.

'If people wanted to stay and keep talking and miss their plane, they could. There was no time pressure.'

Defection numbers will likely rise with Burke expected to provide further details on Wednesday.

The minister confirmed that some players had been in contact with the families back home.

'I haven't been here for each and every conversation but I do know of specific instances where people have spoken to family members, where people have been on the phone,' he said.

'Sometimes there's been a flurry back and forth trying to find the right number, and the numbers have been found and the conversations have happened.

'And people know how Australia's position has been: if you want to stay and wait, you can stay and wait, but it's up to you. People have made their own decision and I think you have to respect that.'

A number of players were 'tearful' and embraced one another as they boarded the flight, while anti-regime protesters at Sydney Airport broke into cheers on hearing that more members of the squad had sought asylum.

Concerns had also been raised after footage emerged on Tuesday of one player being dragged by the hand to leave their Gold Coast hotel.

The players caught a flight out of Sydney to Kuala Lumpur and then onto the Middle East

A group of Iranians living in Australia claimed there has been immense pressure for the women to return to Iran.

While confined to a hotel in Brisbane and under the watch of officials from the Islamic Republic, one team member reportedly responded to a message from the group and claimed 'they have taken all our families hostage,' The Australian reported.

That pressure was seemingly on display when protesters gathered outside the team's hotel in hopes of stopping their bus from reaching Gold Coast Airport.

Witnesses recalled seeing staff dragging teammates onto the bus.

Extra Australian Federal Police officers as well as government officials were stationed at the airport to support the women in case they decided to accept the humanitarian visas at the last minute.

Alongside them were demonstrators urging the women to stay in Australia.

The team and their staff touched down in Sydney about 8.15pm on a flight from the Gold Coast and disembarked behind all other passengers about 8.30pm.

They were accompanied by 12 AFP officers onto a waiting bus.

Passenger Courtnee, who was on the team's flight from the Gold Coast to Sydney, said the players had boarded the Qantas flight first.

Demonstrators in Sydney urged the women to accept the visas

A message, reportedly from one of the team mates, claimed they would not accept the visas as their families were being held 'hostage'

Protesters and police greeted the women at Sydney International Airport (above)

Demonstrators gathered at Sydney Airport to show their support for the women

Passenger Courtnee (above), who was aboard the team's transfer flight from the Gold Coast to Sydney, told Daily Mail the captain made a heartwarming speech

Dozens of supporters gathered at the entrance of Sydney International Airport on Tuesday night

She told Daily Mail that many travellers were touched by the flight's 'beautiful atmosphere', created in part by the captain's touching tribute to the players.

'The captain made a really beautiful announcement about how important it is we look after each other and the situation in the world at the moment,' Courtnee said through tears.

'The mood on the plane was really nice, everyone applauded the pilot and everyone was nice and really great.'

The players were 'keeping composed, keeping together' in the first few rows of economy seating.

'People waved goodbye, said things. I said "I wish you the best luck with your lives",' Courtnee said.

A total of 14 heavily-armed police officers - eight women and six men - waited alongside the team for their international flight.

It's understood their first leg is an eight-and-a-half-hour trip to Kuala Lumpur.

The flight, MH140, had been due to take off at 10.15pm but was delayed.

Earlier on Tuesday protesters attempted to stop a bus leaving the players' Gold Coast hotel

One player appeared to be led by the hand onto the bus by a teammate

Demonstrators showed their solidarity at Sydney Airport on Tuesday night

It's understood players Golnoosh Khosravi and Mohadeseh Zolfi, both in their early 20s, were not on the plane and had taken up the visa offer along with a team procurement manager, Fleur Meshkin-Kar.

The five players earlier granted asylum have been identified as Fatemeh Pasandideh, Zahra Ghanbari, Zahra Sarbali, Atefeh Ramazanzadeh and Mona Hamoudi.

Minister Burke gave a press conference at Sydney Airport after the plane left in which he said each player was given time alone with a Home Affairs official, away from minders on the team, to discuss if they wanted to stay in Australia.

'A choice was given and it was in a situation where there was no pressure,' he said.

'I respect the comments and concerns people have had with the protests around... but before they got on that plane each of them had an oppourtunity to make their own decision and I respect their decision.'

Sports.title: President Donald Trump will still 'welcome' Iran at the World Cup, FIFA chief claims - despite ongoing conflict in the Middle East
Sports.content:
FIFA president Gianni Infantino has claimed Donald Trump has offered reassurances Iran are welcome to compete in the World Cup despite the ongoing war.

Iran have qualified for this summer's tournament, but their participation has been plunged into doubt following US and Israeli air strikes on the country - leading to the death of the country's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Iran responded to the strikes by launching attacks on US-allied states in the Gulf, further escalating the conflict.

When asked about Iran's participation at the World Cup by Politico last week, US President Trump replied: 'I really don't care'.

The head of Iran's football federation, Mehdi Taj, recently stated it is 'unlikely that we can look forward to the cup' amid the conflict, with suggestions the country could withdraw.

But Infantino, who awarded Trump the first FIFA Peace Prize in December, has now claimed the US President has reiterated that Iran are still welcome to compete.

Donald Trump has reportedly reassured FIFA President Gianni Infantino that Iran will be welcome to play at this summer's World Cup

Trump last week was quoted as saying 'I really don't care' when asked about Iran's participation amid the ongoing conflict

The US and Israeli began air strikes on Iran in February with the conflict having escalated with Iran targeting US-allied states in the Gulf in response

Trump reportedly made the assertion in a meeting with the FIFA President on Tuesday.

'This evening, I met with the President of the United States, Donald J Trump to discuss the status of preparations for the upcoming FIFA World Cup, and the growing excitement as we are set to kick off in just 93 days,' Infantino said in a statement.

'We also spoke about the current situation in Iran, and the fact that the Iranian team has qualified to participate in the FIFA World Cup 2026.

'During the discussions, President Trump reiterated that the Iranian team is, of course, welcome to compete in the tournament in the United States.

'We all need an event like the FIFA World Cup to bring people together now more than ever, and I sincerely thank the President of the United States for his support, as it shows once again that Football Unites the World.'

Iran are currently scheduled to play all of their three group matches in the United States.

They would open their campaign against New Zealand at the SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles on June 15, before playing Belgium at the same venue six days later.

Iran are then due to complete the group stage in Seattle on June 26 against Egypt.

Iran secured their place in a fourth successive World Cup by finishing top of Group A in Asian qualifying, marking them as one of the strongest teams in the AFC.

The team was slated to be based at the Kino Sports Complex in Tucson, Arizona, for the duration of the event, but those plans are now in significant jeopardy.

Sports.title: Cheltenham tips: Thursday's best bets for EVERY race from our top tipster Newsboy
Sports.content:
Cheltenham tips: Thursday's best bets for EVERY race from our top tipster Newsboy

Newsboy's race by race guide includes a 1-2-3 for seven races on day three of the four-day Cheltenham Festival which has the Stayers' Hurdle as its feature

View 7 Images Newsboy has made his picks for the Cheltenham Festival (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

OLDSCHOOL OUTLAW (1.20) beat Bambino Fever when the pair met at Naas in December - and there’s less between the pair than the betting might have us believe. Placed in bumpers in the first three months of 2025 for Co Kildare-based Garry Caldwell, Oldschool Outlaw joined Gordon Elliott after finishing seventh at Aintree in April.

‌



Walk In The Park’s daughter started out for her new stable in a Listed maiden hurdle for mares at Navan in November and scored by six and a half lengths. She then met last year’s Champion Bumper heroine Bambino Fever in a maiden hurdle at Naas and came out on top by half a length, the front two 11 lengths clear of the third.

‌



Cheltenham festival jockey suspended over false starts on first day READ MORE :

That performance caught the eye of JP McManus and Oldschool Outlaw carried the green-and-gold livery when completing her path to today with a nine-length supremacy over Place De La Nation in the Grade 3 Solerina Mares Novice Hurdle at Fairyhouse at the beginning of last month.

‌



There is plenty more where that came from. Hot favourite Bambino Fever, with Festival ‘previous’ on her CV, has to be feared, with Echoing Silence and Charme De Faust the most convincing candidates for the final place on the podium.

NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 OLDSCHOOL OUTLAW, 2 BAMBINO FEVER, 3 ECHOING SILENCE

View 7 Images Jordans Cross: Newsboy's pick (Image: PA)

‌



JORDANS CROSS (2.00) is unbeaten over fences when staying upright, and another good account is on the cards.

Successful in a point-to-point at Quakerstown in Co Clare in March 2024, the son of Berkshire started out for Anthony Honeyball with a second in a Chepstow bumper nine months later.

Having gone one better in a maiden hurdle at Southwell in December, Jordans Cross ran second under a penalty at Doncaster two days before the turn of the year and was course for 301 days before going chasing this season.

‌



My selection marked his debut in the sphere with a two-and-a-half-length winning margin at Aintree at the end of October and was challenging for the lead when coming down at the final fence behind Kdeux Saint Fray at Cheltenham weeks later.

But he was back in business with a nose call from Go West at Doncaster 12 days before Christmas. And Jordans Cross made it three wins from three completed starts over the larger obstacles with another minimum-margin verdict from Quebecois in the novices’ handicap chase over course and distance on Cheltenham’s Trials Day card in January. Sam Twiston-Davies’ partner is another 6lb higher today, but has more to give and must go well.

In an open, full-field contest, Meetmebythesea looks primed to go well, while Wingmen and Sixmilebridge are other names to throw into the melting pot.

‌



NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 JORDANS CROSS, 2 MEETMEBYTHESEA, 3 WINGMEN

View 7 Images Wodhooh: fancied for the Mares' Hurdle (Image: PA)

WODHOOH (2.40) saw her burden lightened when Lossiemouth went for the Champion Hurdle - and should make no mistake.

‌



Gordon Elliott’s mare has been a winning machine over hurdles - she’s lost just once in 10 starts in the sphere.

A maiden from four attempts on the Flat for Sir Michael Stoute, Wodhooh was promoted to first place when making her obstacles debut at Listowel in September 2023 but needed no further intervention from the stewards to finish her juvenile campaign a perfect 5-5.

Sent into handicaps last season, my selection carried on the good work with a beating of Joyeuse over this course and distance 11 days before Christmas 2024 and returned to the Cotswolds to lift the Martin Pipe Conditional Jockeys’ Handicap Hurdle here last March, thumping Act Of Authority by four and a quarter lengths.

‌



A first defeat at the hands of Lossiemouth in the Grade 1 Aintree Hurdle the following month was no disgrace, but normal service has been resumed this term.

Wodhooh marked her comeback by landing the Grade 2 Ascot Hurdle in November and yet another personal best - a two-and-a-quarter-length margin from Feet Of A Dancer in a Leopardstown Grade 3 over Christmas - gives Jack Kennedy’s mount a class edge.

Last year’s runner-up Jade De Grugy looks destined for the silver medal once more, with Feet Of A Dancer and Take No Chances fighting it out for the bronze.

‌



NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 WODHOOH, 2 JADE DE GRUGY, 3 FEET OF A DANCER

View 7 Images Ma Shantou: Stayer's Hurdle pick (Image: Alan Crowhurst, Getty Images)

MA SHANTOU (3.20) takes on the toughest assignment of his career - but is expected to prove up to the job.

‌



Emma Lavelle handled the career of 2019 Stayers’ Hurdle hero Paisley Park with consummate skill - and in Ma Shantou the trainer has another top grinder on her hands. Successful in two of his five attempts as a novice last season - he scored at Warwick and

Windsor in the run-up to Christmas 2024 - Shantou’s son finished the campaign with a seventh to Jasmin De Vaux in the Albert Bartlett Novices’ Hurdle at the Festival 12 months ago.

But the seven-year-old has made great strides this winter, returning to action with a handicap score under Harry Cobden here in October.

‌



Ma Shantou was below par when favourite for a similar event at Haydock Park four weeks later, but since then it has been gravy all the way.

Staying in the handicap sphere, my selection bounced back to beat Ace Of Spades over this course and distance on New Year’s Day.

And he made light of the move back up in class to land a punt in the Grade 2 Cleeve Hurdle on Cheltenham’s Trials Day fixture at the end of January, storming up the hill to hold Impose Toi by seven lengths.

‌



The evidence of that performance is that Ma Shantou isn’t finished yet, and he’s the choice for a fourth - and most important yet - victory at the home of National Hunt racing.

In a stellar running of the Stayers’ Hurdle, Teahupoo, first and second in 2024 and 2025, reigning champion Bob Olinger and Honesty Policy command respect, along with regular attender Home By The Lee.

NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 MA SHANTOU, 2 TEAHUPOO, 3 HONESTY POLICY

‌



View 7 Images Fact To File can defend the Ryanair Chase

FACT TO FILE (4.00) is rightly a warm order to keep hold of the crown he captured in bloodless fashion 12 months ago.

Many will perceive a lack of ambition in the move to come back to the Ryanair Chase rather than seek glory in the Gold Cup - but fortune doesn’t always favour the brave.

‌



Fact To File spared his backers an anxious moment when striding away to justify 6-4 favouritism in this race last March, and it should be a similar story for the Willie Mullins trainee this time around.

Although he was well below form behind Marine Nationale back at two miles at Punchestown in April, JP McManus’s nine-year-old lost nothing in defeat but first prize when a neck second to stablemate Gaelic Warrior in the Grade 1 John Durkan Memorial Punchestown Chase on his return.

FREE William Hill Shop Bets inside your Mirror every day of Cheltenham Don't miss a FREE shop bet inside your favourite racing paper every day of the Cheltenham festival! Plus, get expert inside from our top tipsters with pullouts every day from Tuesday March 10 - Friday March 13, 2026 covering the runners, riders, and everything you need to make the 102nd Cheltenham festival the biggest ever. Find out more here. Bet 18+ only. Terms and conditions apply. See paper for details.

‌



My selection didn’t shine when sixth behind The Jukebox Man in the top-level King George VI Chase at Kempton Park on Boxing Day, but his win in the Irish Gold Cup at Leopardstown last time out is right up there with his career highlights.

Never far from the action, Mark Walsh’s mount strode to the front on the home turn and wasn’t for catching, extending his lead to five lengths from Gaelic Warrior by the time he got to the finish line.

An emphatic winner of the Brown Advisory Novices’ Chase here two years ago, Fact To File should rack up a Festival hat-trick.

‌



King George second Banbridge deserves to be high on the list of dangers, followed by Jonbon and last year’s runner-up Heart Wood.

NEWBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 FACT TO FILE, 2 BANBRIDGE, 3 JONBON

View 7 Images Absolutely Doyen: has been a revelation for Paul Nicholls (Image: PA)

‌



ABSOLUTELY DOYEN (4.40) is unbeaten in five starts for Paul Nicholls - and there’s no reason why he shouldn’t show to the fore today.

Doyen’s son failed to find the winner’s enclosure in five outings in point-to-points and three starts over hurdles for Fergal O’Brien.

But the switch to Paul Nicholls has seen a new horse emerge, starting with a victory at odds of 1-7 against three opponents at Stratford in October.

‌



Absolutely Doyen doubled up in a Sedgefield novice hurdle the following month before stepping into the handicap sphere to rack up the hat-trick at Ascot.

The six-year-old kept up the winning run when landing a qualifier for today at Wincanton on Boxing Day, and the nap hand arrived via a three-and-three-quarter-length humbling in a novices’ hurdle at Musselburgh - Absolutely Doyen carried an 8lb penalty - last time.

This test is obviously more demanding, but it will be a surprise if the 14-time champion trainer can’t tease out further progress from this going-places performer.

‌



In a headscratcher of a Pertemps Final, favourite Supremely West, C’Est Different and the first-time-blinkered Melbourne Shamrock are other names to throw into the mix.

NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 ABSOLUTELY DOYEN, 2 SUPREMELY WEST, 3 MELBOURNE SHAMROCK

View 7 Images Herakles Westwood is Newsboy's choice (Image: Getty Images)

‌



HERAKLES WESTWOOD (5.20) brings plenty of Cheltenham form to the table - and the booking of top amateur Finny Maguire is another plus.

Warren Greatrex saddled the nine-year-old to novice handicap chase wins at Newbury and Windsor last season, but Saddler Maker’s son, seventh in the 2025 National Hunt Chase before a fifth at Punchestown, has been kept to the home of jump racing in three outings since.

Interestingly, Maguire was on board when Herakles Westwood made his return in a handicap chase for amateur riders here in November and ran second to Hung Jury.

‌



My preference lost a shoe but came home well with a fourth to Blaze The Way over this course and distance 13 days before Christmas, but was back to winning form over a furlong shorter here on New Year’s Day.

Sent off at 11-2 in a field of eight, Herakles Westwood had more in hand than the official margin of victory might suggest when holding Katate Dori by half a length.

Under the circumstances, a 2lb nudge up the handicap could have been worse. In a fiendishly difficult close to Wednesday’s fun, Waterford Whispers, Daily Present, Weveallbeencaught and Ask Brewster are just four horses who merit a second look.

Article continues below

NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 HERAKLES WESTWOOD, 2 WATERFORD WHISPERS, 3 WEVEALLBEENCAUGHT

Sports.title: Cheltenham jockeys furious as Ruby Walsh calls for immediate intervention - 'It's a joke'
Sports.content:
Cheltenham jockeys furious as Ruby Walsh calls for immediate intervention - 'It's a joke'

Ruby Walsh and several jockeys expressed frustration with race starts at Cheltenham Festival Day 2 on Wednesday, with the racing legend calling for the British Horseracing Authority to step in

View 2 Images Ruby Walsh wants to see a change at Cheltenham (Image: ITV Racing)

Multiple jockeys voiced their dissatisfaction with race starts at the Cheltenham Festival on Wednesday. The second day of the prestigious meeting was marred by complaints from riders about the starters' procedures before each race.

‌



King Rasko Grey, trained by Willie Mullins and ridden by Paul Townsend, triumphed in the opening Novices' Hurdle. Harry Cobden then guided Kitzbuhel to victory in the second race of the day, the Novices' Chase, securing consecutive wins for Mullins to begin proceedings.

‌



Jingko Blue subsequently claimed the Handicap Hurdle before Final Orders prevailed in the Cross Country Chase. Throughout the afternoon, however, there was evident discontent amongst jockeys regarding the starters' instructions at the beginning of races.

‌



ITV were covering the second day of the festival, with presenter Matt Chapman disclosing that he had spoken to several jockeys following their races. Chapman shared comments from four jockeys who had apparently spoken about the race starts at the festival.

Cheltenham horse HMS Seahorse dies as Festival rocked by tragic news READ MORE :

Cheltenham Festival jockey row explodes as amateur claims 'abuse' in remarkable TV rant READ MORE :

"They let him go when we weren't ready, it's just a joke," Chapman claims Mark Walsh said, who finished 14th aboard Free Spirit in the Novices' Hurdle.

‌



Chapman then added that Jack Kennedy said: "It's too tight at the start. There are too many runners, it's too tight and we're being packed together."

Brian Hughes reportedly had stern words about the race starts on day two. Chapman reported that Hughes told him: "Horrific, horrendous. The outside horses are cantering in, everyone on the inside are jogging in, the ones on the inside basically have no chance."

View 2 Images There have been frustrations with the start line at Cheltenham (Image: Tom Jenkins, Getty Images)

‌



Gavin Sheehan, jockey for Wendigo who fell in the Brown Advisory Novices' Chase, allegedly told Chapman: "We are saying no and the starter says go."

Racing icon Ruby Walsh echoed the sentiments of the jockeys and urged the British Horseracing Authority to intervene.

"The BHA [British Horseracing Authority] need to give their starters the right mechanisms to start races," Walsh told ITV. "A quarter of this field had no chance."

Sports.title: Value Scope: Each way horseracing tips from Steve Jones for Cheltenham Festival Thursday on ITV
Sports.content:
Value Scope: Each way horseracing tips from Steve Jones for Cheltenham Festival Thursday on ITV

The ace tipster has another four recommendations from day three of the four day Cheltenham Festival

View Image Steve Jones has tips for the Cheltenham Festival on ITV

The future is bright for the Ryanair Mares’ Novices Hurdle Willie Mullins won this Grade 2 contest in each of the first five years after it was introduced to the Festival but hasn’t been successful since 2020.

‌



The champion trainer has half a dozen runners this year – including hot favourite Bambino Fever – but I prefer FUTURE PROSPECT (1.20). A tendency to pull too hard in the early stages held her back in four bumpers races but she settled much better when making an impressive hurdles debut on her return to action at Naas in January. Her jumping was good and she seemed much happier being ridden prominently. Have 1pt each-way at 20-1. Plenty of bookies are paying out on four places.

‌



Cheltenham Festival jockey row explodes as amateur claims 'abuse' in remarkable TV rant READ MORE :

Queen Camilla sports flamboyant hat at Cheltenham's Ladies Day with Carole Middleton READ MORE :

WINGMEN (2.00) looks ready to take off in the Jack Richards Novices’ Handicap Chase. He put it up to smart Final Demand on his chase debut at Navan and he’s twice run sound races since.

‌



Gordon Elliott’s runner was a decent novice hurdler last season. He twice hit the frame in Grade 1 contests and he has a touch of class. A well-run 2m4f on decent ground looks ideal. Have 1pt each-way at 12-1. Most firms are offering five places.

CLASSIC KING (4.40) hasn’t quite hit top form this season but he looks primed for the Pertemps Network Final. He won over hurdles and fences last season before signing off for the summer with an excellent Kempton second behind smart Aston Martini.

‌

Sports.title: Cheltenham races result: Il Etait Temps survives scare to win for England World Cup winner
Sports.content:
Cheltenham races result: Il Etait Temps survives scare to win for England World Cup winner

Willie Mullins claimed the Queen Mother Champion Chase for the family of Craig Kieswetter, who earned man of the match honours in the 2010 ICC World Twenty20

View 2 Images It Etait Temps wins the Champion Chase under Paul Townend (Image: PA)

Il Etait Temps survived a final fence scare to claim big race honours for the family of an England World Cup winner. The Willie Mullins-trained grey was the clear second choice in the betting for the BetMGM Queen Mother Champion Chase behind JP McManus-owned stablemate Majborough, at 5-6 one of the supposed bankers of the week.

‌



Yet after getting to the front, Majborough made a litany of errors which were to take their toll as he dropped away leaving Paul Townend to make his winning move on Il Etait Temps. The pair led over the second last and quickened away only for a stumble at the last which nearly halted his momentum but they recovered well to pull away again for a ten length win.

‌



Cheltenham horse HMS Seahorse dies as Festival rocked by tragic news READ MORE :

Cheltenham Festival jockey row explodes as amateur claims 'abuse' in remarkable TV rant READ MORE :

The 50-1 chance Libberty Hunter, a first runner in the name of Cath Williams whose husband Evan gave up his licence after being convicted of assaulting a dog walker with a hockey stick, finished best to fill second place.

‌



Ii Etait Temps is owned by Hollywood Racing and Barnane Stud, which is run by Craig Kieswetter and his family. Kieswetter was man of the match in the England cricket-team which won the ICC World Twenty20 in 2010.

He said: “It’s incredible. We’ve had a few attempts. I’m a bit speechless to be honest. We’ve all flown over from South Africa. You can never question Willie and Jackie and it’s great to be a part of their team.”

View 2 Images Craig Kieswetter: played 71 times for England's white ball team (Image: Getty Images)

‌



It Etait Temps was winded when he took a heavy fall at Ascot in January but recovered to claim his seventh Grade 1 victory.

“I thought they were going to fast and it’s fantastic to have a jockey like Paul that knows they’re going too fast,” said Mullins.

“As they were coming down the hill at the third last I could see Paul getting more confident in the saddle.

Article continues below

“We all got a heart attack at the last but Paul had him in a good position and that bit of confidence he had not to follow the pace was crucial to me in winning the race”

The trainer added: “Majborough can certainly gallop and certainly jump but today it fell apart.”

Sports.title: Lewis Hamilton criticised by George Russell as radio message not picked up by TV revealed
Sports.content:
Lewis Hamilton criticised by George Russell as radio message not picked up by TV revealed

George Russell won the Australian Grand Prix for Mercedes but untelevised team radio shows safety concerns about former team-mate Lewis Hamilton and Ferrari's Charles Leclerc

View 2 Images Lewis Hamilton was among the drivers criticised by George Russell (Image: Getty Images)

Previously unreleased team radio footage has revealed Australian Grand Prix winner George Russell criticising the strategies of Ferrari pair Lewis Hamilton and Charles Leclerc. Russell and his teammate Kimi Antonelli secured a Mercedes one-two finish at Albert Park, with Leclerc and Hamilton coming in third and fourth respectively.

‌



One incident during the race Down Under sparked controversy when, after pitting on lap 12, Russell found himself catching up to the yet-to-stop Hamilton on Lap 28. The seven-time former world champion defended robustly from his ex-teammate on the high-speed approach to Turn 9.

‌



The manoeuvre blocked Russell as he moved to the inside, although it didn't prevent him from reclaiming the lead into the corner. Now, unbroadcasted team radio has disclosed that Russell was less than pleased with Hamilton's defence. He stated: "So much movement in the straight when these guys are defending. It's mega dangerous."

‌



Sky Sports confirm F1 pundits for 2026 season as controversial big name missing READ MORE :

F1 is back so make sure you don't miss a thing with our free enhanced coverage READ MORE :

Earlier, Russell was heard grumbling about Hamilton's teammate, Leclerc, during their fight for the lead in the initial laps of Sunday's race in Melbourne. The Monegasque was seen pushing Russell to the inside of the track as the Mercedes prepared a move at Turn 11.

In another broadcasted radio transmission, the 28-year-old said: "That was very dangerous by Leclerc, especially [after] what we spoke about in the briefing."

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

View 2 Images Australian Grand Prix winner George Russell criticised Lewis Hamilton

Russell has also recently confessed that he is preparing himself for 'illegal' allegations from Hamilton ahead of the Chinese Grand Prix. This comes amidst an ongoing discussion about the new engine regulations, with several drivers in the paddock voicing their concerns about the contentious changes - a matter Mercedes are unsurprisingly comfortable with after dominating the Aussie GP.

Article continues below

He stated: "I'm flying with Lewis, so I'm sure I'm going to hear 'your engine is so good, your compression ratio is illegal' and this and that. So yeah, I think I'll just try and get to sleep quite early."

When asked how he would react if Hamilton did make those jibes, the Mercedes driver humorously replied: "I'd just say 'shut up and focus on your own stuff'."

Sports.title: Bayer Leverkusen troll Arsenal hours before Champions League clash after 'boring' criticism
Sports.content:
Bayer Leverkusen troll Arsenal hours before Champions League clash after 'boring' criticism

Arsenal will take on Bayer Leverkusen in the Champions League with the German side seeing the funny side of their opponent's quality from corners as they bid to stop them

View Image Arsenal have mastered the art of the set piece - which remains a huge talking point

Bayer Leverkusen has seen the funny side of Arsenal's set piece genius by placing a sign that said "no corners allowed" ahead of their Champions League clash.

‌



The German side will host the Premier League leaders on Wednesday, hoping to secure an upset victory that would give them a chance of progressing beyond the last 16. They will need to contain Arsenal and, particularly, their quality from dead ball situations.

‌



Arsenal's tactics and route to goal has been widely criticised, but the Bundesliga outfit saw the funny side and posted a picture of their sign covering the corner spot and included the words: "Worth a try".

‌



The north Londoners sit top of the table in England, have a perfect record in Europe and are in contention for both domestic cups. It means the quadruple is still on the cards, even if their methods have proved very divisive.

Nearly 38 percent of their 58 league goals have stemmed from balls into the box, be that from corners of free-kicks. Tottenham frontman Mathys Tel has criticised the tactic and claimed that is makes for very boring viewing.

Arsenal legend Thierry Henry raises doubts on former club in Champions League prediction READ MORE :

Arsenal's double transfer plan to hamper young star as England World Cup door closes READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



In an interview with Zack Nani the Spurs star said: “I’ll tell you the truth, yes, it’s not exciting. It’s boring to watch; it’s really just a clash between two teams with their own ideas.

“There’s less spectacle. There’s no Vinicius pulling off a sombrero flick, no dribble, no Kylian [Mbappe] accelerating past you. Here, I’d say it’s more structured, maybe too much so. All those set-pieces, little details that can sometimes make the difference. But sometimes you think it might be too much. I told the assistant coach in charge of set-pieces, ‘Don’t put me on that one,’ because it’s a zoo.”

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



Arsenal don't see it that way with their players hailing the work of set piece coach Nicolas Jover. Ex-Leverkusen star Piero Hincapie said: "We pay attention to the details. Our set-piece strength is talked about all over Europe. That’s the result of daily training with our set-piece coach, Nico Jover."

The Gunners are favourites to claim the Champions League and have been placed on the so-called easier side of the draw. If they can get past Leverkusen they will face either Sporting Lisbon or Bodo/Glimt in the quarter-final.

Arteta knows that he cannot face the likes of Barcelona, PSG, Bayern Munich, Real Madrid or Manchester City until the final.

Sports.title: Igor Tudor has proved a Tottenham disaster - and not sacking him NOW is unfathomable
Sports.content:
Igor Tudor has proved a Tottenham disaster - and not sacking him NOW is unfathomable

The Tottenham rot was started last summer by the decision of Daniel Levy and the board to sack Ange Postecoglou even after the Australian had won the Europa League

View 3 Images Igor Tudor has lost all four of his games in charge of Tottenham Hotspur

The only thing more bizarre than the Igor Tudor appointment is the fact that he is still at Tottenham Hotspur. To parachute in a coach for his 12th job in 11 years - a coach with a long but unimpressive CV and with no experience of English club football - at a watershed moment of Spurs’ Premier League season was an unfathomable decision.

‌



That this surreal cameo has lasted four matches, and looks like being extended, is even more unfathomable. If there was one inescapable conclusion to be taken from the defeat in Madrid, it was that whoever is out there as a possible replacement cannot do any worse than the Croatian.

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Yes, it has been that disastrous. And the disaster began when Daniel Levy sacked Ange Postecoglou, simple as that.

Revisionism? Quite possibly. But this was a manager who had just won a major trophy. This was a manager who had not lost the confidence and trust of the players.

This was a manager who was not disliked by a majority of the fanbase. Yes, the bottom three were dreadful but Postecoglou’s Spurs had 34 points after 29 games last season and, at this stage, had long been safe.

‌



Tudor’s Spurs have 29 points from 29 games and it will be 29 points from 30 games after the visit to Anfield. Postecoglou’s win percentage as Tottenham manager was 46.5 percent - not spectacular but not overly shabby.

View 3 Images Harry Redknapp said he would be happy to do a short-term job at Spurs before they appointed Igor Tudor

Levy, of course, is no longer involved in the decision-making processes at Spurs but Vina Venkatesham is. And the chief executive had this to say when appointing Frank.

‌



“We defined ten characteristics that we think are important to be a successful manager at Tottenham Hotspur. We analysed in real detail, though our technical staff, more than 30 candidates.”

Did they do the same when they knew they were going to get rid of Frank? It does not look like they had a clue what they were going to do, quite frankly.

Surely a good technical staff would identify a coach - even an interim one - who could at least implement some defensive organisation. Under Tudor, they have scored five and conceded 14. Surely a good chief executive would realise that motivation will play a part in what is now a Premier League relegation battle.

‌



View 3 Images Ange Postecoglou took Spurs to Europa League glory in his second season as manager (Image: (Photo by JUSTIN TALLIS/AFP via Getty Images) )

When ex-professionals were queuing up to suggest Harry Redknapp should step in after Frank was sacked, I thought the idea was laughable. Love Harry but he was about to turn 79.

And then I spent a bit of time with Redknapp at Ben Pauling’s stables, where The Jukebox Man has been prepared for his crack at Friday’s Cheltenham Gold Cup. And, seriously, I came away KNOWING Harry would do a better job than Tudor is doing.

Article continues below

The Jukebox Man has got a good chance of giving Redknapp a memorable day but the chances of him being given a memorable return to Spurs are slim and none. But he would still be a better bet than Tudor.

Sports.title: Arsenal's double transfer plan to hamper young star as England World Cup door closes
Sports.content:
Arsenal's double transfer plan to hamper young star as England World Cup door closes

Arsenal's star studded squad has put them in the position to land multiple honours, but the amount of quality has seen Myles Lewis-Skelly's opportunities decrease

View 2 Images Myles Lewis-Skelly has found starts hard to come by at Arsenal

Arsenal youngster Myles Lewis-Skelly enjoyed a breakout campaign last term, but this season has slipped down the pecking order - with his situation potentially worsening as a result of the club's transfer plans.

‌



The teenager made 39 appearances in all competitions last term, proving himself in defence and midfield, with his standout moment coming when he scored against Manchester City at the Emirates.

‌



This year Mikel Arteta has preferred other options, which has limited Lewis-Skelly to just a solitary start in the Premier League. He's been subbed on 13 times, but it represents a sharp fall down the pecking order.

‌



Arsenal's decision to sign Piero Hincapie on loan in the summer has added competition for the Arsenal academy graduate - and football.london understands that the move will be made permanent. Hincapie has jumped ahead of Lewis-Skelly and, if he is going to stay long-term, it leaves the young Englishman needing to usurp him.

Before that he also needs to dispatch of Riccardo Calafiori, who would be deemed above him in the pecking order. Even a move into midfield is an avenue that appears cluttered - with a number of stellar performers fancied ahead of the 19-year-old.

Arsenal legend Thierry Henry raises doubts on former club in Champions League prediction READ MORE :

Arsenal offered Julian Alvarez transfer hope as Atletico Madrid star casts doubt over future READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Declan Rice, Mikel Merino, Martin Zubimendi and even Kai Havertz can all play in the middle of the pitch. It is understood that Arsenal will also look to bring in a midfielder this summer, which would see even more individuals ahead of Lewis-Skelly.

The timing of his situation is unfavourable for the player, who had hoped to push on with England. He has previously been picked by Thomas Tuchel but was left out of the squad in November, when the German claimed he couldn't justify picking the Arsenal teenager given his lack of minutes - a reality that has not changed.

View 2 Images The teenager knows his England hopes are fading due to his lack of minutes

‌



He said: "Myles was a very good team-mate and played for us in the last camp in the World Cup qualifier in Riga, so was Ruben Loftus-Cheek, so was Morgan Gibbs-White. But I said last time there is a component to it and that is about competition and performance and the door is always open for guys who perform on a high level regularly."

Arsenal's transfer activity in recent years has seen them assemble a squad that is regarded among the best in Europe. Their depth has proved a huge asset to Arteta, but it has left certain players on the outer. Ethan Nwaneri, another academy graduate, headed out on loan in January to ensure he could get some minutes.
